context: Prevalence:   7.4 % of breast surgeries ◊  3rd most common breast complaint Classification: communicating cysts (b)  pathologic: benign / malignant neoplasm; galactorrhea ← hyperprolactinemia from a pituitary adenoma (a)  benign (50%): duct ectasia; intraductal papilloma (b)  malignant (15%): papillary carcinoma; ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS); invasive ductal carcinoma Type of discharge: (a)  normal: ›  milky ›  multicolored sticky (blue, green, gray, brown, black) (b)  abnormal: ›  purulent: antibiotics, incision, drainage (c)  surgically significant (in 14.3% cancerous) ›  clear / watery:
